content:
Seems like there's no description for klint on github, could it be set to something?

image.png
Also - could rust-next maybe be set to the default branch on GH, rather than rust, to point users to the intended base? https://rust-for-linux.com/branches also doesn't mention rust-dev

Trevor Gross said:

Seems like there's no description for klint on github, could it be set to something?

image.png

We probably can also fill the website field with https://rust-for-linux.com/klint.

@Gary Guo

Trevor Gross said:

Also - could rust-next maybe be set to the default branch on GH, rather than rust, to point users to the intended base? https://rust-for-linux.com/branches also doesn't mention rust-dev

That means we cannot have a README.md, so we decided a while ago to have a new branch with the GitHub-only stuff like the CI. It is in my backlog.

Trevor Gross said:

Also - could rust-next maybe be set to the default branch on GH, rather than rust, to point users to the intended base? https://rust-for-linux.com/branches also doesn't mention rust-dev

Honestly having an precise explanation on how the branches are organized could be very useful, since certain things could not be clear to someone new to the project: why does the rust branch have extra features compared to the other branches, if the other branches are the ones being actively developed; also, to contribute should someone start from rust-next or rust-dev? rust-dev sounds like a development branch, and also has the latest updates, but it is not documented

Miguel Ojeda said:

Trevor Gross said:

That means we cannot have a README.md, so we decided a while ago to have a new branch with the GitHub-only stuff like the CI. It is in my backlog.

Makes sense, thanks for the explanation

Michele Dalle Rive said:

Trevor Gross said:

Also - could rust-next maybe be set to the default branch on GH, rather than rust, to point users to the intended base? https://rust-for-linux.com/branches also doesn't mention rust-dev

Honestly having an precise explanation on how the branches are organized could be very useful, since certain things could not be clear to someone new to the project: why does the rust branch have extra features compared to the other branches, if the other branches are the ones being actively developed; also, to contribute should someone start from rust-next or rust-dev? rust-dev sounds like a development branch, and also has the latest updates, but it is not documented

https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/tree/MAINTAINERS#n18558, according to MAINTAINERS, rust-next is the branch to start.

rust-dev is not documented since it's currently not a mandatory step in the patch workflow: it's currently used as a patch queue for LGTM patches in case someone wants to develop/test with these patches. But it shouldn't affect anyone's development process, i.e. if you have a conflict with rust-dev, it doesn't matter. And the branch is volatile and subject to changes.

Boqun Feng said:

https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/tree/MAINTAINERS#n18558, according to MAINTAINERS, rust-next is the branch to start.

But actually, since patch submission happens on the mailing list, sometimes the base branch doesn't matter (as long as the patch is post and doesn't conflict with others, and has enough reviews).

Boqun Feng said:

But actually, since patch submission happens on the mailing list, sometimes the base branch doesn't matter (as long as the patch is post and doesn't conflict with others, and has enough reviews).

Indeed, if something does not require what is in rust-next, then one can use one of Linus' tags as the base, which are even more stable.

Michele Dalle Rive said:

Trevor Gross said:

Also - could rust-next maybe be set to the default branch on GH, rather than rust, to point users to the intended base? https://rust-for-linux.com/branches also doesn't mention rust-dev

Honestly having an precise explanation on how the branches are organized could be very useful, since certain things could not be clear to someone new to the project: why does the rust branch have extra features compared to the other branches, if the other branches are the ones being actively developed; also, to contribute should someone start from rust-next or rust-dev? rust-dev sounds like a development branch, and also has the latest updates, but it is not documented

That would be https://rust-for-linux.com/branches (I am a bit confused because the message you reply to mentions it, so I guess you saw it but found it incomplete?).
